title: NOC 60030 – Restaurant and Food Service Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Restaurant and food service managers under NOC 60030 are the operational leaders of Canada's world-class hospitality sector. As Canada’s dining landscape continues to expand—from high-end eateries to fast-casual franchises—the demand for experienced managers remains a high priority. For immigration purposes, this TEER 0 managerial role is a core eligible occupation for Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker) and multiple Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) streams, offering a stable and lucrative pathway to Permanent Residency (PR).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level0Major Group60 – Executive and senior management occupationsSub-Major Group600 – Management occupationsMinor Group6003 – Managers in food service and accommodationNOC Code60030

duties: Plan, organize, and evaluate the operations of a restaurant, bar, or cafeteria.
Recruit staff, oversee training, and monitor employee performance.
Control inventory, monitor revenues, and modify procedures or prices to maximize profit.
Resolve customer complaints and ensure strict adherence to health and safety regulations.
Negotiate arrangements with suppliers for food and beverages.
employment_requirements: Completion of a college or other program related to hospitality or food service management is usually required.
Several years of experience in the food service sector, including supervisory experience, are mandatory.
Responsible beverage service certification is required for managers of establishments serving alcohol.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate to Favourable. Over the next decade, Canada anticipates approximately 32,500 new job openings in this sector. While automation affects some service roles, the human element of management ensures these positions remain indispensable.
labour_market_demand: The Canadian food service industry is currently grappling with a management shortage. In provinces like Ontario and British Columbia, the post-pandemic resurgence has led to an aggressive search for managers who can optimize revenues and lead large teams. Demand is especially concentrated in urban hubs like Toronto, Vancouver, and Montreal.
Competitive Compensation: High base salaries often supplemented by performance bonuses.
Industry Growth: A resilient sector with continuous opportunities for multi-unit management.
Quality of Life: Access to Canada’s premium social services and a safe environment for your family.
pathways: Federal Skilled Worker (Express Entry): High priority for TEER 0 roles.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Direct pathway for managers with a full-time job offer.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A popular choice for those settling in Atlantic Canada.
